trusted_baseline.commit_semantics: Verified GitHub merge commit for PR #10 before the replay-baseline-assessment workstream was created; live GitHub state determines the current main head.

workstreams.objective: Connect replayed timing findings to governed commissioned-baseline resolution and deterministic drift assessment through explicit rule applicability contexts, while preserving temporal-rule status, baseline-envelope status, rejection evidence, and authority boundaries.
verification_criteria: the pull-request diff remains inside permitted_paths
the workstream baseline is the verified PR #10 merge commit
each timing finding is converted to a baseline observation only through an explicit rule context
missing timing context, baseline not found, baseline ambiguity, and comparison rejection remain explicit outcomes
temporal-rule status remains distinct from commissioned-baseline drift and approved-envelope status
machine-readable output preserves rule, asset, correlation, timestamps, delay, resolution evidence, rejection reasons, and retained uncertainty
no finding asserts fault, root cause, safe production change, or authorization

verification_evidence.local_targeted_validation: Syntax and focused replay-baseline tests are validated before publication where local tooling permits.
verification_evidence.evidence_boundary: Local validation is supporting evidence only. Live exact-current-head GitHub CI is authoritative for CI verification after the branch is published.

workstreams.next_gate: Obtain successful exact-current-head CI, perform independent read-only review of the exact head and scope, then require explicit human approval before merge.

authoritative.notes: Owns the deterministic machine-event reasoning core and current LineAlert implementation lineage.

active_related_boundaries.notes: Owns governed evidence-package and transport contracts; it is not merged into LineAlert Core.
Owns containment and policy enforcement; it is not the LineAlert implementation repository.

design_archaeology.disposition: Do not merge or copy wholesale into linealert-core. Reuse requires a new bounded branch, explicit source attribution, current authority boundaries, focused tests, and review.
Not an active implementation source. Preserve only as historical repository evidence.
Preserve as design archaeology. Persistence and retrieval integration require a separately governed current architecture and package boundary.

deployment_state.evidence: No current LineAlert Core deployment evidence is recorded in this repository state.

known_open_pull_requests.title: Assess replay timing against governed baselines
known_open_pull_requests.action: Keep draft pending successful exact-head CI, independent read-only review of that exact head, and explicit human merge approval.
